releases.name: v26.0.0 - Revision 8 + super beta 8xx support
releases.body:
### **UPDATE 2**: Added working a8xx Turnip. **Please read the notes below.**  - Thanks whbexium
### **UPDATE**: Fixed an issue with the drivers not loading properly.

## Based on latest mesa main source v26.0.0-devel.

## v26.0.0 R8
Vulkan 1.4.335
Base commit:  e9a83d530cce

### NOTES

## The a8xx support included is **very** beta and hacky. **Use at your own risk!**  
An example of what might happen (which happened to me) using this with Mincraft Pojav-glow-worm on a840, it will crash and the default driver won't recover causing every apps to crash afterwards. (a reboot fixed it) 

What works very well: OpenGL games through x86_64 emulators.
What does not work: Switch emulators and non OpenGL games (so far tested. They boot but render very buggy graphics).

---

- This still uses Old autotuner. If you need new Autotuner, [use v25.3.0 here](https://github.com/K11MCH1/AdrenoToolsDrivers/releases/edit/v26.0.0-rc.03).

- a710 and 720 can use regular or Gmem. When using regular driver on Winlator, do uncheck sysmem and check gmem from the TU_DEBUG list in the Environment Variables. For other emulators, use Gmem driver.
- For users with the ONE UI bug, use the following environmental variable: `FD_DEV_FEATURES = enable_tp_ubwc_flag_hint=1`

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

### APPLIED PATCHES

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R8_Gmem.zip**
- Added a710/720 GPU descriptors (hacks)

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R8_Autotuner.zip**
- Autotuner Overhaul

**a8xx_0x5_handle.zip**
- Added a830v2 GPU descriptor (hack)
- Added 0x5 handle (hack)
Thanks Hardware Union RU/ENG boys

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Rob Clark
Karmjit Mahil
Connor Abbott
StevenMX
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

releases.name: v26.0.0 - Revision 3 + v25.3.0 with NEW Autotuner
releases.body:
## Based on latest mesa main source v26.0.0-devel.

## v26.0.0 R3
Vulkan 1.4.330
Base commit: 13148afd0

## v25.3.0
Vulkan 14.328
Base commit: 13148afd0
Repo: [PixelyIon/mesa/-/tree/tu-newat](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/PixelyIon/mesa/-/tree/tu-newat)

- Compatible with Winlator Bionic through Adrenotools settings and "Wrapper" graphics driver.
- Compatible with Micewine.  

---

### Notes  

- `Turnip v25.3.0 PREFER_SYSMEM/PROFILED` contains the new Autotuner that is not yet merged to main source. For testing purposes.
- The Turnip drivers above may not work for a710/720 as forcing Gmem is not possible on them.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3` contains old autotuner and gpu a710/720 hacks. Has a perf regression which is fixed with the ones below.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix` contains old autotuner, a perf fix and gpu hacks.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix_Gmem` contains the perf fix, gpu hacks and forced Gmem for a710/720.
- `Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix_Sysmem` contains the perf fix, gpu hacks and forced Sysmem.
- a710 and 720 can use regular or Gmem. When using regular driver on Winlator, do uncheck `sysmem` and check `gmem` from the TU_DEBUG list in the Environment Variables. For other emulators, use Gmem driver.

What is Autotuner

Automatically benchmarks each shader using both sysmem (system memory) and gmem (GPU memory) rendering paths. Over multiple render passes, it measures the execution time of each mode and determines which one consistently performs better. As it gathers more data, it gradually favors the faster mode for that shader, improving performance dynamically in real time. Depending on the workload, it may offer better performance.

**Currently v26.0.0 has the old Autotuner.**

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

### APPLIED PATCHES

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix:** 
- a710/720 support hack
[Fix CB overhead when running without CB being used.](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/merge_requests/38378)

**Turnip_v26.0.0_R3_CB_Perf_Fix_Gmem:** 
- As above + Force Gmem.

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Connor Abbott
StevenMX
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

## Based on latest mesa main source v25.2.0-devel.

Vulkan 1.4.318

Base commit: 7544aae77c

### Compatible with Winlator Bionic through Adrenotools settings and "Wrapper" graphics driver.
### Compatible with Micewine.

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

> [!NOTE]
> <samp> - May not work with DXVK 2.6 and up.  </samp>
> <samp> - `GMEM` for a710 and a720 Winlator users.  </samp>  
> <samp> - `AUTO` for anyone else. However might still need to enable `SYSMEM` on Winlator/Micewine via `TU_DEBUG`</samp>  

> **If full screen artifacing happens, try using the [gmem] tagged driver.**

---

<details>

<summary>📜 What is Autotuner:</summary>

The Autotuner in Mesa's Turnip drivers dynamically selects between GMEM (tile-based, on-chip memory) and sysmem (direct system memory rendering) modes to optimize performance for Adreno GPUs. It uses heuristics to make decisions but can be overridden with TU_DEBUG options for debugging or to address specific issues. While effective in many cases, the Autotuner may struggle with certain workloads or newer GPUs, requiring manual configuration or updated driver variants for optimal performance. Which is why the unsupported GPUs mentioned above may need GMEM.

</details>

---

### MERGED PATCHES

- Hacky a710 and a720.

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

Based on latest mesa main source v25.2.0-devel.

Vulkan 1.4.318

Base commit: eecf94cc

### Compatible with Winlator Bionic through Adrenotools settings and "Wrapper" graphics driver.
### Compatible with Micewine.

---

### CHANGELOG

- Merged upstream.
- Bumped Vulkan version to 1.4.318.
- General optimizations and bug fixes.

---

> [!NOTE]
> <samp> - `GMEM` for a710 and a720 Winlator users.  </samp>  
> <samp> - `AUTO` for anyone else. However might still need to enable `SYSMEM` on Winlator/Micewine via `TU_DEBUG`</samp>  

> **If full screen artifacing happens, try using the [gmem] tagged driver.**

---

<details>

<summary>📜 What is Autotuner:</summary>

The Autotuner in Mesa's Turnip drivers dynamically selects between GMEM (tile-based, on-chip memory) and sysmem (direct system memory rendering) modes to optimize performance for Adreno GPUs. It uses heuristics to make decisions but can be overridden with TU_DEBUG options for debugging or to address specific issues. While effective in many cases, the Autotuner may struggle with certain workloads or newer GPUs, requiring manual configuration or updated driver variants for optimal performance. Which is why the unsupported GPUs mentioned above may need GMEM.

</details>

---

### MERGED PATCHES

- Hacky a710 and a720.

---

### Thanks to ❤️
Pablo
Mark
Weabchan
JeezDisReez
Danylo
Mesa Team

There is no support for this build, this is meant for those who are helping out or want to tinker.

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0 Patch
releases.body:
# Patch & Patch Generation (GameHub v5.1.0)

The files needed to contribute to the project and patch GameHub 5.1.0 yourself are now released. 

- Completely reworked the initial patching approach.
- *Revanced* patch is a work in progress, feel free to contribute to this if you are comfortable with revanced. 
- Easy update patches after modifications 
- Work on macOS/Linux. (maybe windows?)

Read the [README](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/blob/master/README.md) file for a full explanation.

### FAQ

- Does this work with GameHub version *not 5.1.0*
- **no.**


--- 


## How It Works

The patcher uses a multi-step process:

1. **Decompile** - Uses apktool to decompile the original APK to smali bytecode
2. **Delete** - Removes telemetry SDKs, unused assets, and tracking libraries
3. **Patch** - Applies unified diff patches to modify smali code
4. **Add** - Copies new files (resources, additional smali)
5. **Rebuild** - Reassembles the APK using apktool
6. **Sign** - Signs with a debug keystore for installation

## Patch Contents

| Category | Count | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Deletions | 3,385 | Telemetry SDKs, tracking code, unused assets |
| Additions | 2,856 | New resources, modified assets, new features |
| Modifications | 223 | Smali code patches for behavior changes |

### Removed Components

- **Native libraries**: libumeng-spy.so, libcrashsdk.so, libalicomphonenumberauthsdk_core.so, etc.
- **SDKs**: Umeng Analytics, JPush, Firebase Analytics, Tencent login
- **Assets**: Splash video, auth videos, emoji font (saves ~30MB)
- **Permissions**: Location, contacts, phone state, ad tracking

### Added Features

- Custom splash/intro video from community CDN
- Local game ID copy functionality
- Offline mode improvements
- News page integration with community API

## For Developers

### Regenerating Patches

If you've modified the Lite APK and want to update the patches:

```bash
./generate-patches.sh [path/to/original.apk] [path/to/lite.apk]
```

This will:
1. Decompile both APKs
2. Generate diff patches for modified files
3. Copy new files to patches directory
4. Create deletion and addition lists

### Patch Directory Structure

```
patches/
├── files_to_delete.txt        # List of files to remove
├── files_to_add.txt             # List of files to add
├── files_to_patch.txt          # List of files to modify
├── diffs/                                # Unified diff patches
│   ├── AndroidManifest.xml.patch
│   ├── smali/...
│   └── res/...
├── new_files/                        # New files to add
│   ├── res/...
│   └── smali_classes10/...     # Robot language files to add
└── stats.txt                           # Patch statistics (ignored and generated on patch-generation)
```

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0
releases.body:
A restoration build of GameHub Lite V4 before everything fell apart. I will publish the code for the workers and a patch very soon, for now enjoy a working GameHub Lite app again.

We'll focus on restoring the previous functionality first, before fixing and adding functionality. So please only report issues that were not present in GameHub Lite V4 or in the official GameHub app.

---

### Explanation for the different versions

You’ll often see an emulator distribute different versions with a name suffixed like **PUBG**, **Antutu**, **Optimized**, **Genshin**, **Ludashi** and other names. Here follows an explanation for what is what, is still has slight differences between manufacturers and even different packagenames but it should give you a rough understanding.

—-

*The different versions are identical. What we do is a very old android trick to gain extra performance on some devices.*

**Antutu**

Some manufacturers “cheat” by setting the governor to performance when they detect the Antutu package name. 

*Nerd explanation:*

The CPU governor essentially controls the CPU's frequency scaling. allowing it to operate at different clock speeds and voltages based on the system load. So making the CPU go fast for sustained usage, what is actually made for peak usage. This comes with a risk of overheating, but I don’t believe in this. Android does a well enough job of thermal management and makes it extremely hard for software to exceed what the hardware is capable of and damaging itself.


That said, it’s still extra heat. More heat == more bad. I just think it’s negligible, especially if your device has a fan.


**PUBG**

On a high level it’s the same as Antutu, but some slight differences that only benefit games. Think of network prioritization and touch input latency improvements. The manufacturers goal when they detect Antutu is **ALL THE POWER**. Benchmarks are relatively short and it makes them look better on comparison websites.


The goal for PUBG is more like **MORE POWER**, since the intention is often to have a game running for longer it has less aggressive changes.


**TLDR and summary:**

**Antutu** spoofing:

    •    Maximum CPU/GPU frequencies unlocked  
    •    Aggressive performance governors  
    •    Short-duration performance boost (benchmark workload)  
    •    Thermal limits are less strict   
    •    All cores available  


**PUBG** and other games spoofing: 

    •    Sustained gaming performance profiles  
    •    GPU driver optimizations (Adreno/Mali game-specific paths)  
    •    Frame pacing and scheduling improvements  
    •    Reduced touch latency   
    •    Network QoS prioritization  
    •    Different thermal management (sustained vs burst)  
    •    Qualcomm “Game Performance Mode”      
    •    Sometimes enables features like frame-gen


**Ludashi** spoofing:

    •    Similar to Antutu but slightly less aggressive    
    •    Longer sustained performance boost (multi-minute tests)   
    •    Memory frequency optimization

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.0.0
releases.body:
This is a temporary release that restores most of the functionality previously available in GameHub Lite v4.

GameHub Lite v4 stopped working after the former developer took down the Cloudflare Workers it relied on, citing privacy concerns. 

We are currently developing a community-funded solution to provide a more stable and permanent setup.

In the meantime, this version includes most of the core features but may display some visual inconsistencies and lacks certain extras that were present in v4, such as the news and free games sections.

Special thanks to **The412Banner** and **Trixarian**!

[Join the EmuReady Discord for support and more information](https://discord.gg/CYhCzApXav)

releases.name: GameHub Brasil v2.0
releases.body:
# 🎮 GameHub Brasil v2.0 🇧🇷

## 🌟 Destaques desta Versão O GameHub Brasil v2.0 chega para revolucionar como você gerencia seus jogos e drivers. Focamos totalmente na flexibilidade e compatibilidade, permitindo que você importe seus próprios arquivos, gerencie perfis de controle com facilidade e leve a experiência de jogos para dispositivos de qualquer gama de desempenho. --- 🚀 Novidades e Recursos Implementados --- ## 📥AdrenoHub Drivers (Adrenotools). ✅ Integração Adrenotools: Sistema robusto para gerenciamento de drivers Vulkan. 📂 Importação Flexível: Suporte para importação de drivers Turnip no formato .zip. 💾 Fontes Variadas: Carregue drivers de fontes externas ou diretamente do armazenamento local do seu dispositivo. ## 📂 Contents Hub Centralize seus conteúdos de forma prática e rápida. 🎮 Formato .wcp: Importação completa de Contents no formato proprietário. 🔄 Compatibilidade Híbrida: Suporte a arquivos externos e locais. ⚠️ Nota Técnica: O importador de conteúdo está funcional, porém, a importação do **Wine** ainda enfrenta limitações devido a diferenças de arquitetura, estando em aperfeiçoamento. ## 🕹️ Personalização de Controle Jogue do seu jeito, sem precisar configurar tudo do zero. 📥 Importação de Perfis: Adicione perfis de controle personalizados para jogos específicos. 🌐 Fontes Externas: Compatibilidade com perfis baixados da comunidade. 🎯 Praticidade: Aplicação rápida para agilizar o setup dos seus jogos favoritos. ## 💾 Suporte a Armazenamento Externo (OTG) Mais espaço e liberdade para sua biblioteca de jogos. 🔌 Reconhecimento OTG: O app agora detecta e gerencia perfeitamente pen drives e HDs externos conectados via OTG. 📂 Jogar Externo: Instale e rode seus jogos diretamente do armazenamento removível. ## ⚡ LowSpecsExperienceV13 Leve jogos pesados para hardwares mais modestos. 📉 Otimização Gráfica: Programa dedicado a reduzir e otimizar jogos. 🎮 Foco em Jogabilidade: Prioriza FPS e estabilidade em detrimento de efeitos pesados. 📱 Inclusão Total: Já incluído nativamente nesta versão em Componentes. ## ✅ Wrappers e Vortek A ponte entre o sistema Android e seus jogos. 🛠️ Gerenciamento Avançado: Inclusão do sistema de Wrappers e Vortek, similar ao encontrado no Winlator. 🔁 Seleção de Drivers: Permite escolher a camada de compatibilidade ideal para cada título. ## 🔧 Melhorias Técnicas Esta versão focou na arquitetura interna do aplicativo para suportar as novas funções de importação: 🔨 Refatoração do Gerenciador de Arquivos: Melhorias na leitura de caminhos locais e externos. 🛡️ Estabilidade em Importações: Tratamento de erros aprimorado ao ler arquivos .zip e .wcp ## 📥 Informações de Download Nome do Arquivo: GameHub-Brasil-v2.0.apk Requisitos Mínimos: Android 8.0 (Oreo) ou superior 4GB de RAM (Recomendado 6GB+ para uso do LowSpecsExperience) 3GB de espaço livre (Para instalação e drivers) GPU com suporte a Vulkan 1.1+ Dispositivo com suporte a OTG (Opcional, para armazenamento externo) ## 🙏 Agradecimentos Essa versão é um grande passo em direção à personalização. Agradecemos a todos os testadores que validaram as novas funções e algumas que foram importadas do Winlator Ludashi [StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi) E para você ficar por dentro do houve no app, segue o vídeo do nosso parceiro [BrunoAndroid](https://www.youtube.com/@BrunoAndroid) explicando sobre o funcionamento do app:

## 📞 Suporte e Feedback Encontrou algum bug na importação de drivers ou no Contents Hub? 🐛 Reporte problemas na aba [Issues](../../issues) 💡 Compartilhe seus perfis de controle e wrappers na comunidade 💡 Compartilhe ideias em [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@winlatorbrasil) ⭐ Se gostou da atualização, deixe uma estrela no repositório! --- ## 📥 Sobre os pacotes que você deve baixar | Posição (prioridade) | Nome / Categoria | Package name | Motivo de uso (performance / detecção) | |---------------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Futuremark | com.futuremark.dmandroid.application | Benchmark GPU moderno (Wild Life / Solar Bay), referência para modo gamer em vários aparelhos atuais, excelente para GPUs Mali/Adreno. | | 2 | GFXBench | com.gfxbench.glbenchmark | Benchmark gráfico com cenários antigos e atuais (T-Rex, Manhattan, etc.), excelente para GPUs Mali/Adreno. | | 3 | AnTuTu | com.antutu.ABenchMark | Benchmark geral muito popular (CPU + GPU + RAM + UX), frequentemente usado para liberar modo turbo em ROMs chinesas. | | 4 | Ludashi | com.ludashi.benchmark | Benchmark chinês focado em score geral e otimização, conhecido por acionar perfis especiais em alguns devices e ROMs custom. | | 5 | Redmagic | com.riotgames.league.wildrift | Usado por fabricantes para ativar modos de alta performance e alta taxa de quadros. | | 6 | MiHoYo | com.mihoyo.yuanshen | Usado como “teste real” de SoC e gatilho de perfil gamer. | | 7 | PUBG | com.tencent.ig | Tradicionalmente incluído em listas de otimização para reduzir lag e melhorar estabilidade de FPS. | | 8 | GameHub Brasil | com.gamehub.brasil | App genérico/launcher para cenário “normal”, útil para manter um perfil padrão sem overclock ou boost ativado. | ---
**Desenvolvido com ❤️ para a comunidade gaming brasileira** *Jogue mais, jogue melhor!* **V2.0: Mais liberdade, mais jogos!**

releases.name: GameHub Brasil v1.0
releases.body:

--- ## 🌟 Destaques desta Versão Esta versão representa um marco significativo em otimização e estabilidade, trazendo melhorias substanciais de performance para todos os chipsets suportados, além de uma experiência visual completamente renovada e novos componentes de emulação de última geração. --- ## 📦 Novos Componentes e Drivers ### **Box64** - 🔥 **Turnip v26.0.0 R1 Autotuner** - Ajuste automático de performance baseado em GPU - ⚙️ **Turnip v26.0.0 R1** - Versão padrão otimizada --- ## ⚡ Otimizações de Performance por Chipset Esta versão inclui ajustes específicos para extrair o máximo desempenho de cada arquitetura: ### 🔥 **Snapdragon (Qualcomm)** - Otimizações para GPUs Adreno - Perfis customizados para séries 6xx, 7xx e 8xx - Melhor aproveitamento de cache L2/L3 ### 🚀 **Dimensity / Helio (MediaTek)** - Ajustes para arquitetura ARM Mali - Balanceamento de carga entre clusters CPU - Otimização de thermal throttling ### 💫 **Exynos (Samsung)** - Perfis específicos para Mali e Xclipse - Gerenciamento inteligente de frequências - Compatibilidade aprimorada com drivers --- ## 🎨 Renovação Visual Completa ### **Interface Modernizada** - 🖼️ **Novo Wallpaper Principal** - Design moderno e imersivo - 🎬 **Intro Redesenhada** - Animação de inicialização atualizada - 🎨 **Paleta de Cores Renovada** - Esquema cromático mais harmônico e profissional - 🌈 **Wallpapers Adicionais** - Nova coleção de papéis de parede temáticos ### **Seção "Sobre" Aprimorada** - 📝 HTML customizado em "Informações Pessoais" - 💼 Layout mais profissional e organizado - ℹ️ Informações atualizadas e detalhadas --- ## 🌐 Novos Recursos ### **Feed de Notícias & Jogos Grátis** - 📰 Fique atualizado com as últimas notícias do mundo dos games - 🎁 Descubra jogos gratuitos disponíveis - 🔔 Sistema de notificações para novos conteúdos ### **Tradução PT-BR Automática** - 🇧🇷 Interface completamente em Português do Brasil - 🔤 Tradução inteligente de conteúdos dinâmicos --- ## 🔧 Melhorias Técnicas e Correções ### **Otimização de Conectividade** - ❌ **Removida integração direta com GitHub** - Solução definitiva para erro "429: Too Many Requests" - Eliminação de problemas de carregamento do menu ### **Estabilidade Geral** - 🛡️ Correções de bugs críticos - ⚙️ Gerenciamento de recursos otimizado --- ## 📥 Informações de Download **Nome do Arquivo:** `GameHub-Brasil-v1.0.apk` **Requisitos Mínimos:** - releases.name: BannerHub v2.7.4-pre3
releases.body:
## What's new in v2.7.4-pre3

**Fix: HUD overlay now appears automatically when a game starts**

Previously the Winlator HUD bar was only injected into the screen when the in-game sidebar was opened — because `BhFrameRating` was created inside `BhPerfSetupDelegate`, which only attaches when the sidebar inflates. The HUD was invisible until the user manually opened the side menu.

**Fix:** A new `BhHudInjector.injectOrUpdate(Activity)` helper is called from `WineActivity.onResume()` every time the activity resumes. It reads the `winlator_hud` pref and either injects a new `BhFrameRating` into the DecorView (if not already there) or syncs its visibility (if it was added by a previous sidebar open).

No sidebar interaction needed — the HUD appears immediately when the game launches.

---
*Also includes everything from pre2: 3-way API selector, no-root sidebar, draggable HUD, accurate FPS.*

releases.name: BannerHub v2.7.4-pre2
releases.body:
## What's new in v2.7.4-pre2 (since v2.7.3 stable)

### Winlator HUD Performance Overlay
A compact draggable HUD bar injected into the game's DecorView (top-right by default):

**GPU% | CPU% | RAM% | BAT W | TMP °C | FPS**

- Toggle via **Winlator HUD Style** switch in the in-game sidebar → Performance tab
- When enabled, hides the GameHub hudLayer to avoid double overlay
- **Draggable** — tap and drag to reposition anywhere on screen
- FPS reads from GameHub's own `HudDataProvider` (same source as the built-in HUD — accurate averaged FPS)
- GPU% reads from Adreno (`gpubusy` / `gpu_busy_percentage`) or Mali (`utilisation`) sysfs
- CPU% calculated from `/proc/stat` delta between samples (idles correctly across cores)
- Battery wattage from `BatteryManager` current + sysfs voltage
- Temperature from battery sysfs, falls back to thermal zone0
- Updates every 1 second on a background daemon thread

### Performance Sidebar Fixes
- **No more root dialog on sidebar open** — Sustained Perf and Max Adreno Clocks buttons are greyed out unless root has been granted via the app Settings menu (reads `root_granted` pref set by BhRootGrantHelper — never calls `su` from the sidebar)

### 3-way Compatibility API Selector
Replaced the cycle-tap EmuReady toggle with an **AlertDialog** showing all three options as radio buttons:

- **GameHub (Default)** — official GameHub API
- **EmuReady** — EmuReady compatibility API  
- **BannerHub API** — self-hosted (`bannerhub-api.the412banner.workers.dev`)

Selecting an option immediately saves the choice, clears component/token caches, and shows a confirmation toast.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.7.1
releases.body:
> [!WARNING]
> **Only install stable releases (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or CI artifacts.**
> Pre-releases and artifacts are untested intermediate builds used for development. They may be broken, crash, or corrupt your data. Only stable releases have been verified to work correctly.

---

## New since v2.7.0 (last stable)

- **Steam card restored in My Games tab** — v2.7.0 accidentally broke the Steam login/game card by patching the APK versionName to `2.7.0`. GameHub sends versionName in its server API calls; changing it from `5.3.5` triggered a version gate that suppressed the Steam card on all fresh installs. Fixed by keeping the original `5.3.5`.
- **Normal variant is now standalone** — package renamed to `banner.hub`, no longer conflicts with or replaces official GameHub Lite
- **New Normal(GHL) variant** — package `gamehub.lite`, installs in the official GameHub Lite slot for users who want that behavior. APK: `BannerHub-vX.Y.Z-Normal(GHL).apk`

---

## Full Feature Set

### GOG Games Tab
- OAuth2 login via embedded WebView (`GogLoginActivity`)
- Auto token refresh (background thread before expiry)
- Library sync: Gen 2 (content-system depot manifest + chunked CDN download) with Gen 1 fallback (range-download from main.bin) and installer fallback (direct .exe download from api.gog.com)
- Game cards with thumbnail, Gen badge (Gen 1 / Gen 2), and install size
- Install flow: Install button → dialog → ProgressBar + per-file progress text → Add button (opens EditImportedGameInfoDialog to register with launcher)
- ✓ Installed checkmark — shown immediately on completion and persisted across restarts
- Uninstall — removes files, clears prefs, resets card
- Copy to Downloads — copies installed game folder to public Downloads directory

### Component Manager
- Side menu item → ComponentManagerActivity (ListView)
- Backup installed components (ZIP export)
- Inject WCP/ZIP components
- Add New Component (true injection via ComponentInjectorHelper)
- Remove component
- Download from Online Repos — 3-level nav: repo → category (DXVK/VKD3D/Box64/FEXCore/GPU Driver) → filtered asset list → download + inject
  - GitHub Releases API fetch (nightly-* tags)
  - Flat pack.json fetch (Arihany WCPHub)

### BCI Launcher Button
- Top-right toolbar button (`iv_bci_launcher`) opens BannersComponentInjector if installed

### My Games Tab
- Renamed from "My" to "My Games"

### Performance Sidebar
- Sustained Performance Mode toggle (setSustainedPerformanceMode — no root required)
- Max Adreno Clocks toggle (sysfs KGSL min_freq lock — requires root)

### RTS Touch Controls
- Gesture-mapped RTS touch control overlay (credit: @Nightwalker743)

### Offline Mode
- Steam login screen skipped when no network connection — attempts offline launch directly

### CI / Build
- 9 APK variants per stable release: Normal (`banner.hub`), Normal(GHL) (`gamehub.lite`), PuBG, AnTuTu, alt-AnTuTu, PuBG-CrossFire, Ludashi, Genshin, Original
- versionName preserved as `5.3.5` (original GameHub version) to maintain server API compatibility

---

## Installation

Install the APK that matches your use case:

| APK | Package | Use when |
|-----|---------|----------|
| `BannerHub-v2.7.1-Normal.apk` | `banner.hub` | Standalone install — coexists with official GameHub Lite |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.1-Normal(GHL).apk` | `gamehub.lite` | Replaces official GameHub Lite slot |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.1-PuBG.apk` | `com.tencent.ig` | PuBG device slot |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.1-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | AnTuTu slot |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.1-Genshin.apk` | `com.mihoyo.genshinimpact` | Genshin slot |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.1-Ludashi.apk` | `com.ludashi.aibench` | Ludashi slot |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.1-Original.apk` | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | Original slot |

All APKs are signed with the same test key. You can install multiple variants simultaneously as they use different package names.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.7.0
releases.body:
## BannerHub v2.7.0

> [!WARNING]
> **Only install APKs from stable releases (tagged `vX.Y.Z`). Do NOT install pre-release builds (tagged `vX.Y.Z-pre` or `vX.Y.Z-betaN`).**
> Pre-releases are active development snapshots — they may contain unfinished features, broken pipelines, untested code paths, or instability. Stable releases are fully tested, signed (v1/v2/v3), and verified to have passed a clean CI build with correct versionName and app labels. Never install APKs from workflow run artifacts either — those are raw unsigned CI outputs.

### New since v2.6.3 (last stable)
- **GOG Games tab** — full integration via side menu (ID=10) → GogMainActivity
  - OAuth2 login via WebView, session stored in `bh_gog_prefs` SharedPreferences
  - Auto token refresh (GET-based)

  - GOG game launch: reads install path from `bh_gog_prefs`, launches via BCI launcher

### Full Feature Set

- **Components side menu** — ComponentManagerActivity: backup, WCP/ZIP inject, Add New Component (true injection via ComponentInjectorHelper), Remove

- **RTS touch controls** — credit @Nightwalker743
- **GOG Games tab** — full OAuth2 + library sync + install + launch pipeline (see above)
- **Grant Root Access** — Settings button (contentType=0x64) to grant root permissions
- **Sustained Performance** + **Max Adreno Clocks** toggles in Performance sidebar
- **Offline Steam login skip**
- **Per-variant package names** — install alongside other GameHub variants without conflict

### Installation
Choose the APK matching your use case:

| APK | Package | App Label |
|-----|---------|-----------|
| `BannerHub-v2.7.0-Normal.apk` | `banner.hub` | BannerHub |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.0-Normal.GHL.apk` | `gamehub.lite` | BannerHub |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.0-PuBG.apk` | `com.tencent.ig` | BannerHub PuBG |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.0-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | BannerHub AnTuTu |
| `BannerHub-v2.7.0-alt-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | BannerHub AnTuTu |

| `BannerHub-v2.7.0-Original.apk` | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | BannerHub Original |

**Which APK do I need?**
- No GameHub installed → use **Normal** (`banner.hub`) — installs alongside official GameHub Lite without conflict
- Have official GameHub Lite (`gamehub.lite`) and want BannerHub to replace it → use **Normal.GHL**

All APKs are signed with AOSP testkey (v1/v2/v3). App label and versionName are set correctly per variant.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.6.3
releases.body: ## New since v2.6.1 (last stable) ### Component Manager — Complete UI Overhaul The Component Manager has been rebuilt from a plain ListView to a card-based RecyclerView with a polished dark UI: - **Cards** — each component shown as a compact card with a color-coded type badge (DXVK / VKD3D / Box64 / FEXCore / GPU Driver / WCP) and matching left accent strip - **Source badge** — components downloaded via BannerHub's downloader show the repo name they came from on the card (e.g. "Arihany WCPHub", "Nightlies") - **Correct type labels** — type override stored at download time ensures cards always show the right label (FEXCore, DXVK, VKD3D, etc.), even for WCP files from Arihany where the filename alone doesn't tell the type - **Live search bar** — type to filter cards in real time - **Swipe LEFT** to remove a component; **Swipe RIGHT** to back it up - **Header** with ← back button, component title, install count badge, and ✕ All button - **Bottom bar** with **+ Add New** and **↓ Download** buttons - **Empty state** — friendly placeholder shown when no components are installed - **Auto-refresh** — list updates immediately when returning from the download screen ### ✓ Downloaded Indicator In the online repo asset lists, components already installed via BannerHub are marked with ✓. The mark clears automatically when the component is removed (single remove or Remove All). ### Remove All — Correct Count The "Remove All Components" confirmation dialog now counts only BannerHub-managed components (those injected or downloaded via the Component Manager), not all GameHub system components. Remove All also only removes these managed components. ### Compact Cards Component cards are ~20% smaller — more components visible per screen. --- ## Full Feature Set ### Component Manager - Card-based RecyclerView UI with live search, swipe-to-remove/backup, source + type badges - Add New Component — inject WCP or ZIP as a new component slot - Inject file — replace an existing component's contents - Backup — copy component folder to Downloads/BannerHub/ - Remove / Remove All (BannerHub-managed only) ### In-App Component Downloader - 3-level navigation: repo → category → asset list - ✓ downloaded indicator (cleared on removal) - Download progress spinner with filename status text - 6 built-in sources: Arihany WCPHub, The412Banner Nightlies, Kimchi, StevenMXZ, MTR, Whitebelyash ### BCI Launcher Button Top-right toolbar shortcut to BannersComponentInjector ### Performance Sidebar Toggles - Sustained Performance Mode (CPU governor) - Max Adreno Clocks (GPU min=max freq lock) Both persist across launches; require root for full effect ### Grant Root Access (Settings → Advanced) 5-point warning dialog; runs `su -c id`; stores result in bh_prefs; prevents unsolicited root popup on every sidebar open ### Component Descriptions in Game Settings Picker Locally installed components show their description text in the per-game component selection menus ### RTS Touch Controls (credit: @Nightwalker743) - Tap to click, drag for box select, long press for right-click, double-tap, two-finger pan, pinch-to-zoom - Gesture settings dialog (gear icon in Controls tab) ### VRAM Limit Unlock Adds 6 GB / 8 GB / 12 GB / 16 GB options to per-game VRAM limit picker ### Per-Game CPU Core Affinity Multi-select dialog for fine-grained core assignment (Core 0–7) ### Offline Steam Launch Skips Steam login screen when autoLogin fails and no network is available ### Offline Mode for PC Game Settings Component and container lists fall back to empty data offline instead of blocking the menu ### UI Tweaks - "My" tab renamed to "My Games" --- ## Installation Download the APK matching your existing GameHub package: | APK | Package | |---|---| | `Bannerhub-5.3.5-Revanced-Normal.apk` | `banner.hub` (installs alongside GameHub) | | `Bannerhub-5.3.5-Revanced-PuBG.apk` | `com.tencent.ig` | | `Bannerhub-5.3.5-Revanced-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | | `Bannerhub-5.3.5-Revanced-alt-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | | `Bannerhub-5.3.5-Revanced-Ludashi.apk` | `com.ludashi.aibench` | | `Bannerhub-5.3.5-Revanced-Genshin.apk` | `com.mihoyo.genshinimpact` | | `Bannerhub-5.3.5-Revanced-Original.apk` | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | The **Normal APK** (`banner.hub`) coexists with the original GameHub — no uninstall needed. All APKs signed with AOSP testkey (v1/v2/v3).
